vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/auto-start
vBRIEFInfo.description: Auto-synthesized minimal plan for PAN-1143: complete-planning endpoint silently no-ops when canonical spec doesn't exist yet (false success)

plan.title: complete-planning endpoint silently no-ops when canonical spec doesn't exist yet (false success)
plan.author: panopticon-cli/auto-start

narratives.Problem:
## Symptom

`pan plan done <id>` (and the dashboard "Done" button) report **success** even when the canonical spec was never written and beads were never materialized. The user sees a green check, agent gets started, work agent has nothing to do (or has 1 placeholder bead instead of the planned 22).

Concrete reproduction (PAN-1141, 2026-05-16):
- Planning agent (auto mode) wrote `workspaces/feature-pan-1141/.pan/spec.vbrief.json` with 22 plan items
- No canonical spec created at `<projectRoot>/.pan/specs/`
- `pan plan done PAN-1141` returned: `✓ Planning complete for PAN-1141`
- After: still no canonical spec, beads still show only 1 generic `PAN-1141: Implement issue` placeholder
- Work agent spawned successfully and loaded "1 task"

## Root cause

`src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts:1044-1053`:

```ts
const { pushed: gitPushed, beadsWarning } = yield* Effect.promise(async () => {
  if (!projectPath) return { pushed: false, beadsWarning: null };

  const workspacePath = join(projectPath, 'workspaces', `feature-${issueLower}`);
  const workspacePlanPath = findPlan(workspacePath);   // ← returns null on first run

  let gitRoot = '';
  if (workspacePlanPath) gitRoot = workspacePath;

  if (!gitRoot) return { pushed: false, beadsWarning: null };  // ← bails silently
  // ... spec promotion + bead sync never runs
```

`findPlan(workspacePath)` resolves via `findSpecByIssue(projectRoot, issueId)` in `src/lib/vbrief/io.ts:44`, which scans **only** `<projectRoot>/.pan/specs/`. There is no workspace-draft fallback. So on the **first** finalize attempt — when the canonical spec by definition doesn't yet exist — `findPlan` returns null and the entire promotion-and-bead-sync block is skipped. The endpoint returns `{ success: true }` regardless.

Additionally: the catch on line 1117-1119 around `writeSpecForIssue` swallows any promotion error as "non-fatal" with only a `console.warn` — so even if the path executed, errors wouldn't surface to the caller.

## Why this is critical

The documented planning lifecycle is: workspace draft → `pan plan-finalize` → `pan plan done`. The first link is broken because **`pan plan finalize` also calls `findPlan(workspace)` and fails with "No workspace spec found"** for the same reason — it expects the canonical spec to already exist, which it can't on first run.

Net effect: the auto-plan path silently produces unusable artifacts. Bug-by-bug:
1. **First-run chicken-and-egg.** `findPlan` requires canonical spec; canonical spec requires successful finalize; finalize requires canonical spec.
2. **Silent success.** Both `pan plan finalize` ("✓ No workspace spec found" is `process.exit(1)`, fine) and `complete-planning` (returns 200 with `success: true`) fail to surface the missing-canonical case usefully to the operator.
3. **Work agent runs on stub beads.** `pan start` happily spawns an agent with 1 placeholder bead, leading to "agent has nothing to do" or "agent does ill-defined mega-task" failures downstream.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] **First-run path works.** When `complete-planning` runs and no canonical spec exists, it reads the workspace draft at `<workspace>/.pan/spec.vbrief.json`, validates it, and uses it as the source for `writeSpecForIssue(...)`. The canonical spec gets written, beads get materialized, the issue advances. No more silent bail at line 1053.
- [ ] **Same for `pan plan finalize`.** If invoked when canonical spec doesn't exist, it should accept the workspace draft as the source.
- [ ] **No more silent error swallow.** Remove or narrow the catch at line 1117-1119 — promotion failures must propagate to the HTTP response so the CLI fails loudly with the real error.
- [ ] **Work-agent start validates beads count > 1.** Or validates plan items match bead count. Refuses to spawn with the stub placeholder.
- [ ] **Planning role doc is accurate.** `roles/plan.md` step 6 references `pan plan-finalize` (hyphenated, doesn't exist as a separate command). Align doc with actual CLI: `pan plan finalize`.
- [ ] **Test coverage.** Integration test for the first-run path: create a workspace draft, call complete-planning, assert canonical spec written + N beads created.

## Out of scope

- Reworking the four-artifact model in `docs/VBRIEF.md` — the model is fine; the implementation diverges from it.
- Adding new planning modes.

## Why this matters operationally

I just spent ~20 minutes of context tracing why PAN-1141 had 1 bead instead of 22. A first-time user would hit this on their first non-trivial plan, get an unusable workspace, and have no idea why. This is the kind of bug that destroys trust in the planning pipeline.

## Related

- PAN-1124 (single-spec-on-main model) — the architecture that introduced this. The model is sound; the first-run case wasn't wired up.
- PAN-1140 (workspace init container fix) — concurrent in-flight work, no direct overlap.

## Workaround until fixed

Manually re-run the planning agent for the affected issue. If it fails the second time the same way, that's a deterministic bug — file a comment with the agent's transcript.

The canonical spec gets written, beads get materialized, the issue advances. No more silent bail at line 1053.
**Same for pan plan finalize.** If invoked when canonical spec doesn't exist, it should accept the workspace draft as the source.
**No more silent error swallow.** Remove or narrow the catch at line 1117-1119 — promotion failures must propagate to the HTTP response so the CLI fails loudly with the real error.
**Work-agent start validates beads count > 1.** Or validates plan items match bead count. Refuses to spawn with the stub placeholder.
**Planning role doc is accurate.** roles/plan.md step 6 references pan plan-finalize (hyphenated, doesn't exist as a separate command). Align doc with actual CLI: pan plan finalize.
**Test coverage.** Integration test for the first-run path: create a workspace draft, call complete-planning, assert canonical spec written + N beads created.
